description: This map displays an example of a Natura 2000 (N2K) site, illustrating its enlargement between 2012 and 2018. The base imagery is a false-colour infrared satellite view, where dense vegetation (forests) appears dark red, agricultural fields are lighter pink/red, and urban/built-up areas are cyan. A blue line outlines the 2 km buffer zone of the N2K site as defined in 2012. Yellow-coloured polygons indicate areas that were added to the Natura 2000 database in 2018, representing the site's enlargement. These newly included yellow areas are predominantly situated outside the 2012 blue buffer, especially visible in the southern and western parts of the map. The map includes a scale bar of "1:50.000" with a segment length of "2 km" and a north arrow.
